title: urtica dioica subvar. glabrata
intro: Urtica dioica subvar. glabrata, commonly known as Stinging Nettle, is a subspecies of the perennial Urtica dioica plant that is found in damp environments and has reduced stinging properties due to its glabrous (hairless) leaves. This plant has been used for centuries in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antihistamine, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Urtica dioica subvar. glabrata include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Stinging Nettle include teas, capsules, and topical creams, which can be used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
